message:
Fix BD_ACTOR handling in direct mode paths

BUG: BD_ACTOR env var was only read in --no-db mode (lines 352-360)
and daemon RPC path. Normal direct mode and JSONL-only mode detection
paths fell back to $USER without checking BD_ACTOR.

Fix: Add explicit os.Getenv("BD_ACTOR") check in both:
- JSONL-only mode detection path (lines 393-402)
- Normal direct mode path (lines 440-450)

(gt-6r18e.2)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-0vg","title":"Pinned issues: persistent context markers","description":"Add ability to pin issues so they remain visible and are excluded from work-finding commands. Pinned issues serve as persistent context markers (handoffs, architectural notes, recovery instructions) that should not be claimed as work items.

Use Cases:
1. Handoff messages - Pin session handoffs so new agents always see them
2. Architecture decisions - Pin ADRs or design notes for reference  
3. Recovery context - Pin amnesia-cure notes that help agents orient

Core commands: bd pin, bd unpin, bd list --pinned/--no-pinned","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:33:10.911092-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:30:28.989696-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:30:28.989696-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-0vtq","title":"Investigate JSONL hash mismatch warnings (bd-160)","description":"During v0.39.1 release, saw repeated warnings:

WARNING: JSONL file hash mismatch detected (bd-160)
  This indicates JSONL and export_hashes are out of sync.
  Clearing export_hashes to force full re-export.

This appeared multiple times during wisp close operations. Questions:
1. What causes export_hashes to drift from actual JSONL content?
2. Is this expected during wisp operations (since wisps do not export)?
3. Should wisp operations skip the hash check entirely?
4. Is there actual data integrity risk or just noise?

The warning references bd-160 - may need to check that issue for context.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:51:25.448856-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-27T23:22:09.589444-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T23:22:09.589444-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-14v0","title":"Add Windows code signing for bd.exe releases","description":"## Context

Go binaries (including bd.exe) are commonly flagged by antivirus software as false positives due to heuristic detection. See docs/ANTIVIRUS.md for full details.

## Problem

Kaspersky and other AV software flag bd.exe as PDM:Trojan.Win32.Generic, causing it to be quarantined or deleted.

## Solution

Implement code signing for Windows releases using:
1. An EV (Extended Validation) code certificate
2. Integration with GoReleaser to sign Windows binaries during release

## Benefits

- Reduces false positive rates over time as the certificate builds reputation
- Provides tamper verification for users
- Improves SmartScreen trust rating on Windows
- Professional appearance for enterprise users

## Implementation Steps

1. Acquire EV code signing certificate (annual cost ~$300-500)
2. Set up signtool or osslsigncode in release pipeline
3. Update .goreleaser.yml to sign Windows binaries
4. Update checksums to include signed binary hashes
5. Document signing verification in ANTIVIRUS.md

## References

- docs/ANTIVIRUS.md - Current documentation
- bd-t4u1 - Original Kaspersky false positive report
- https://github.com/golang/go/issues/16292 - Go project discussion","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:46:48.459177-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T23:54:41.912141-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T23:54:41.912141-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14v0","depends_on_id":"bd-t4u1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:47:02.024159-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8x43","title":"Add 'bd where' command to show active beads location","description":"## Problem

When using redirects, it's unclear which beads database is actually being used. This caused debugging confusion during the v0.39.0 release.

## Proposed Solution

Add `bd where` command:

```bash
bd where
# → /Users/stevey/gt/beads/mayor/rig/.beads (via redirect from crew/dave/.beads)

bd where --json
# → {"path": "...", "redirected_from": "...", "prefix": "bd"}
```

## Alternatives Considered

- `bd info --beads-path` - but `bd where` is more discoverable
- Just fix the UX so it's never confusing - defense in depth is better

## Desire Paths

This supports the 'desire paths' design principle: make the system's behavior visible so agents can debug and understand it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-27T21:15:52.666948-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:27:50.301295-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T21:27:50.301295-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-8y9t","title":"Remove bd mol spawn - use pour/wisp only","description":"Remove the spawn command from bd mol. Replace with:

- bd pour <proto> - Instantiate as mol (liquid, persistent)
- bd wisp <proto> - Instantiate as wisp (vapor, ephemeral)

Rationale:
- 'spawn' doesn't fit the chemistry metaphor
- Two phase transitions (pour/wisp) are clearer than one command with flags
- Avoids confusion about defaults

Implementation:
1. Add bd pour command (currently bd mol spawn --pour)
2. Add bd wisp command (currently bd mol spawn without --pour)
3. Remove bd mol spawn
4. Update all docs/tests

Gas Town tracking: gt-ingm.6","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:40:51.698189-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T12:52:55.307-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T12:52:55.307-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-8zbo","title":"Code smell: runCook function is ~275 lines","description":"attached_args: Refactor runCook function (~275 lines)

The runCook() function in cmd/bd/cook.go (lines 83-357) is ~275 lines handling:

1. Flag parsing and validation
2. Mode determination (compile vs runtime)
3. Formula parsing and resolution
4. Control flow, advice, and expansion application
5. Dry-run output
6. Ephemeral mode JSON output
7. Persist mode database operations
8. Result display

Consider extracting:
- parseAndValidateFlags() - flag handling
- loadAndResolveFormula() - parse, resolve, apply transformations
- outputDryRun() - dry run display logic
- outputEphemeral() - ephemeral JSON output
- persistFormula() - database persistence logic

The function has multiple exit points and nested conditionals that make it hard to follow.

 {"id":"bd-9115","title":"CLI cleanup: Consolidate bd top-level commands","description":"## Problem
bd has 76 top-level commands, making it hard to discover and remember.

## Proposed Changes

### Remove from top-level (nest instead):
- `bd cook` → `bd formula cook` (cooking is a formula operation)
- `bd pin` → already covered by `bd mol` commands
- `bd thanks` → `bd info --thanks` or just README
- `bd quickstart` → `bd help quickstart` or docs
- `bd detect-pollution` → `bd doctor --check=pollution`

### Consolidate migrations under `bd migrate`:
- `bd migrate-hash-ids` → `bd migrate hash-ids`
- `bd migrate-issues` → `bd migrate issues`
- `bd migrate-sync` → `bd migrate sync`
- `bd migrate-tombstones` → `bd migrate tombstones`

### Consolidate admin under `bd admin`:
- `bd cleanup` → `bd admin cleanup`
- `bd compact` → `bd admin compact`
- `bd reset` → `bd admin reset`

## Backwards Compatibility
Keep old commands as hidden aliases for one release cycle.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-27T14:28:03.299469-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-27T16:19:44.580326-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T16:19:44.580326-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-w3z7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:44.88081-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-do8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:44.905058-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-3u8m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:44.929207-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-x0zl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:44.953799-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-wb9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:44.977559-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-twbi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:45.002403-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-kff0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:45.027177-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-bxqv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:45.051629-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9115","depends_on_id":"bd-5e9q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:45.07537-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-95k8","title":"Pinned field available in beads v0.37.0","description":"Hey max,

Heads up on your mail overhaul work:

1. **Pinned field is available** - beads v0.37.0 (released by dave earlier) includes the pinned field on issues. You'll want to add this to BeadsMessage in types.go.

2. **Database migration** - Check if existing .beads databases need migration to support the pinned field. Run `bd doctor` to see if it flags anything.

3. **Sorting task** - Once you have the pinned field, gt-ngu1 (pinned beads first in mail inbox) needs implementing. Since messages now come from `bd list --type=message`, you'll need to either:
   - Sort in listBeads() after fetching, or
   - Ensure bd list returns pinned items first (may already do this?)

Check what version of bd you're building against.

-- Mayor","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:51:57.315956-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.542169-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.542169-08:00","labels":["from:beads-crew-dave","thread:thread-71ac20c7e432"]}

 {"id":"bd-a3sj","title":"RemoveDependency fails on external deps - FK violation in dirty_issues","description":"In dependencies.go:225, RemoveDependency marks BOTH issueID and dependsOnID as dirty. For external refs (e.g., external:project:capability), dependsOnID doesn't exist in the issues table. This causes FK violation since dirty_issues.issue_id has FK constraint to issues.id.

Fix: Check if dependsOnID starts with 'external:' and only mark source issue as dirty, matching the logic in AddDependency (lines 162-170).

Repro: bd dep rm <issue> external:project:capability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:44:51.981138-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:48:29.062424-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:48:29.062424-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-a3sj","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:44:51.982343-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-a9y3","title":"Add composite index (status, priority) for common list queries","description":"SearchIssues and GetReadyWork frequently filter by status and sort by priority. Currently uses two separate indexes.

**Common query pattern (queries.go:1646-1647):**
```sql
WHERE status = ? 
ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at DESC
```

**Problem:** Index merge or full scan when both columns are used.

**Solution:** Add migration:
```sql
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status_priority ON issues(status, priority);
```

**Expected impact:** Faster bd list, bd ready with filters. Particularly noticeable at 10K+ issues.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:50.515275-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.838976-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.838976-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-a9y3","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:50.516072-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ecmd","title":"Add type: event for state transitions","description":"Events capture operational state changes as immutable records.

Fields needed:
- event_type: string (namespaced category like patrol.muted, agent.started)  
- actor: string (entity URI who caused this)
- target: string (entity URI or bead ID affected)
- payload: object (event-specific JSON data)

Example:
  id: evt-x7k2
  type: event
  event_type: patrol.muted
  actor: human:overseer
  target: agent:deacon
  payload: {reason: fixing deadlock, until: gt-abc1}

This enables:
- bd activity --follow showing events
- bd list --type=event --target=agent:deacon
- Full audit trail for operational state
- HOP-compatible transaction records","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","created_at":"2025-12-30T15:15:23.440095-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:54:09.224-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T15:54:09.224-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented event type with event_kind, actor, target, and payload fields"}

 {"id":"bd-eko4","title":"Add pre-migration orphan cleanup to avoid chicken-and-egg fix failure","description":"## Problem

When the database has orphaned foreign key references (dependencies or labels pointing to non-existent issues), the migration invariant check fails, preventing the database from opening. This creates a chicken-and-egg problem:

1. `bd doctor --fix` tries to open the database
2. Opening triggers migrations with invariant checks
3. Invariant check fails due to orphaned refs
4. Fix never runs because database won't open

## Root Cause

Aggressive tombstone deletion (e.g., deleting ~1000 old tombstones) left behind orphaned:
- 218+ dependencies where issue_id not in issues
- 222+ dependencies where depends_on_id not in issues  
- 30+ labels where issue_id not in issues

## Current Workaround

Direct SQL to clean up before bd can run:
```sql
DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM issues WHERE id = issue_id);
DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM issues WHERE id = depends_on_id) AND depends_on_id NOT LIKE 'external:%';
DELETE FROM labels WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM issues WHERE id = issue_id);
```

## Proposed Fix

Add a pre-migration cleanup step that runs BEFORE invariant checks:
1. Option A: Run orphan cleanup as part of migrations (before invariant check)
2. Option B: Add `bd doctor --force-fix` that bypasses invariant checks
3. Option C: Have invariant check auto-clean orphans instead of failing

Option A is cleanest - orphan cleanup should be part of normal migration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T23:18:24.65227-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-29T13:46:30.625571-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T13:46:30.625571-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented Option A: CleanOrphanedRefs runs before captureSnapshot in RunMigrations, preventing chicken-and-egg problem"}
 {"id":"bd-elqd","title":"Systematic bd sync stability investigation","description":"## Context

bd sync has chronic instability issues that have persisted since inception:
- issues.jsonl is always dirty after push
- bd sync often creates messes requiring manual cleanup
- Problems escalating despite accumulated bug fixes
- Workarounds are getting increasingly draconian

## Goal

Systematically observe and diagnose bd sync failures rather than applying band-aid fixes.

## Approach

1. Start fresh session with latest binary (all fixes applied)
2. Run bd sync and carefully observe what happens
3. Document exact sequence of events when things go wrong
4. File specific issues for each discrete problem identified
5. Track the root causes, not just symptoms

## Test Environment

- Fresh clone or clean state
- Latest bd binary with all bug fixes
- Monitor both local and remote JSONL state
- Check for timing issues, race conditions, merge conflicts

## Success Criteria

- Identify root causes of sync instability
- Create actionable issues for each problem
- Eventually achieve stable bd sync (no manual intervention needed)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T22:57:25.35289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"bd-gr4q","title":"Gate await fields cleared by auto-flush/auto-import cycle","description":"## Problem

When creating a gate with `--await` fields (e.g., `gh:run:123`), the await_type and await_id are initially stored correctly in the SQLite database. However, they get cleared (set to empty strings) during subsequent bd commands due to the auto-flush/auto-import cycle.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create a gate with await:
   ```
   bd gate create --molecule test-mol --await gh:run:20517738002
   ```
   Output shows correct `await_type: gh:run, await_id: 20517738002`

2. Check database - fields are present

3. Run any bd command (e.g., `bd list`)

4. Check database again - await_type and await_id are now empty

## Root Cause

Gates are wisps (ephemeral issues). During the auto-flush cycle:
1. Auto-flush exports issues to JSONL (filtering out wisps)
2. Auto-import reads JSONL and updates DB rows
3. The update clears fields that weren't in the JSONL (including await fields)

## Workaround

Using `--no-auto-import --no-auto-flush` flags preserves the fields, but this isn't practical for normal use.

## Impact

GitHub gate evaluation (`bd gate eval`) cannot work because by the time eval runs, the await fields have been cleared.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Don't clear await fields during auto-import if they weren't in the source
2. Store wisp fields separately from the main JSONL export
3. Include wisps in export but filter them at sync time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:59:11.309657-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T23:20:20.33151-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T23:20:20.33151-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-gxq","title":"Simplify bd onboard to minimal AGENTS.md snippet pointing to bd prime","description":"## Context
GH#604 raised concerns about bd onboard bloating AGENTS.md with ~100+ lines of static instructions that:
- Load every session whether beads is being used or not
- Get stale when bd upgrades
- Waste tokens

## Solution
Simplify `bd onboard` to output a minimal snippet (~2 lines) that points to `bd prime`:

```markdown
## Issue Tracking
This project uses beads (`bd`) for issue tracking.
Run `bd prime` for workflow context, or hooks auto-inject it.
```

## Rationale
- `bd prime` is dynamic, concise (~80 lines), and always matches installed bd version
- Hooks already auto-inject `bd prime` at session start when .beads/ detected
- AGENTS.md only needs to mention beads exists, not contain full instructions

## Implementation
1. Update `cmd/bd/onboard.go` to output minimal snippet
2. Keep `--output` flag for BD_GUIDE.md generation (may still be useful)
3. Update help text to explain the new approach","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:42:38.604891-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T11:47:28.020419-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T11:47:28.020419-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-h0we","title":"Review SQLite indexes and scaling bottlenecks","description":"Audit the beads SQLite schema for:

## Index Review
- Are all frequently-queried columns indexed?
- Are compound indexes needed for common query patterns?
- Any missing indexes on foreign keys or filter columns?

## Scaling Bottlenecks
- How does performance degrade with 10k, 100k, 1M issues?
- Full table scans in hot paths?
- JSONL export/import performance at scale
- Transaction contention in multi-agent scenarios

## Common Query Patterns to Optimize
- bd ready (status + blocked_by resolution)
- bd list with filters (status, type, priority, labels)
- bd show with dependency graph traversal
- bd sync import/export

## Deliverables
- Document current indexes
- Identify missing indexes
- Benchmark key operations at scale
- Recommend schema improvements","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:41:06.481881-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:25.178175-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:25.178175-08:00"}

**Context:** GitHub issue #479 - users sometimes get beads into an invalid state after updates, and there's no clean way to start fresh. The git backup/restore mechanism that protects against accidental deletion also makes it hard to intentionally reset.

bd daemons killall

 {"id":"bd-j4cr","title":"bd cook: waits_for field not preserved in cooked protos","description":"The `waits_for` field in formula steps is not preserved in the cooked proto.

## Formula

```yaml
- id: aggregate
  title: Aggregate arm results
  needs: [survey-workers]
  waits_for: all-children
  description: |
    This is a **fanout gate**...
```

## Expected

The cooked proto step should have metadata indicating it's a gate:
- Label: `gate:all-children` or
- Field in issue metadata indicating wait behavior

## Actual

The `waits_for` field is silently ignored. Only the description mentions 'fanout gate'.

## Impact

The execution engine cannot determine which steps are gates that need to wait
for dynamically-bonded children. This breaks the Christmas Ornament pattern
where aggregate steps must wait for all polecat-arm wisps to complete.

## Related

bd-hr39: needs field not converted to step dependencies","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:51:10.494565-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.932552-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.932552-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-jdz3","title":"Add pager support to bd list","description":"Add pager support following gh cli conventions:

Flags:
- --no-pager: disable pager for this command

Environment variables:
- BD_PAGER / PAGER: pager program (default: less)
- BD_NO_PAGER: disable pager globally

Behavior:
- Auto-enable pager when output exceeds terminal height
- Respect LESS env var for pager options
- Disable pager when stdout is not a TTY (pipes/scripts)","notes":"## Implementation Plan

### Dependencies
```go
import "github.com/muesli/termenv" // or golang.org/x/term
```

### Code Changes

1. **Add pager helper** (internal/ui/pager.go):
```go
func ToPager(content string) error {
    // Check BD_NO_PAGER or --no-pager
    if os.Getenv("BD_NO_PAGER") \!= "" {
        fmt.Print(content)
        return nil
    }
    
    // Get pager command
    pager := os.Getenv("BD_PAGER")
    if pager == "" {
        pager = os.Getenv("PAGER")
    }
    if pager == "" {
        pager = "less"
    }
    
    // Check if content exceeds terminal height
    // If not, just print directly
    
    // Pipe to pager
    cmd := exec.Command(pager)
    cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(content)
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    return cmd.Run()
}
```

2. **Add --no-pager flag** (cmd/bd/list.go init):
```go
listCmd.Flags().Bool("no-pager", false, "Disable pager output")
```

3. **Use pager in list output** (end of Run):
```go
if \!noPager && isTerminal(os.Stdout) {
    ui.ToPager(output.String())
} else {
    fmt.Print(output.String())
}
```

### Environment Variables
- `BD_PAGER`: pager program (overrides PAGER)
- `BD_NO_PAGER`: set to any value to disable
- `PAGER`: fallback pager
- `LESS`: passed through for less options

### Testing
- `bd list` pipes to pager when output > terminal height
- `bd list --no-pager` prints directly
- `BD_NO_PAGER=1 bd list` prints directly
- `bd list | cat` auto-disables pager (not a TTY)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:09.109258-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.356435-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:41:45.168318-08:00","close_reason":"Merged via refinery"}

 {"id":"bd-nim5","title":"Detect/prevent child→parent dependency anti-pattern","description":"## Problem

When filing issues with dependencies, it's easy to fall into the "temporal trap":
- Thinking "Phase 1 comes before Phase 2"
- Writing `bd dep add phase1 phase2`
- But that means "phase1 depends on phase2" (backwards!)

A specific variant: children depending on their parent epic. This is ALWAYS wrong because:
1. Parent-child relationship already captures the dependency (parent closes when children done)
2. Child→parent dep creates a block: child can't start because parent is "open"
3. Parent can't close because children aren't done
4. Deadlock

## Solution

### 1. Prevent at creation (`bd dep add`)

When user runs `bd dep add X Y`:
- Check if X is a child of Y (X.id starts with Y.id + ".")
- If so, error: "Cannot add dependency: X is already a child of Y. Children inherit dependency on parent completion via hierarchy."

### 2. Detect in `bd doctor`

Add a check that finds all cases where:
- Issue A depends on Issue B
- A.id matches pattern B.id + ".*" (A is child of B)

Report as: "Child→parent dependency detected (anti-pattern)"
Offer repair: "Remove N backwards dependencies? [y/N]"

## Implementation

- `dep_add.go`: Add parent-child check before adding dependency
- `doctor.go`: Add backwards-dep detection to health checks

When done, commit and push to main.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T20:01:13.586558-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T20:06:11.099142-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T20:06:11.099142-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-nkqx","title":"bd swarm validate command","description":"Add bd swarm validate to check epic structure for swarming.

## Context
Before running a swarm, we need to validate the epic structure:
- Are dependencies correct (not inverted)?
- Are there orphaned issues?
- Is the DAG well-formed?

The Ready Front model (not phases!) means dependencies define execution order.
Common mistake: 'Phase 1 before Phase 2' → inverted deps.
Correct thinking: 'Phase 2 needs Phase 1' → bd dep add phase2 phase1

## Implementation
1. New command: bd swarm validate <epic-id>
2. Walk the dependency DAG from epic children
3. Detect issues:
   - Root issues with no dependents (orphans?)
   - Leaf issues depending on nothing (missing deps?)
   - Cycles (impossible)
   - Disconnected subgraphs
4. Report:
   - Ready fronts (waves of parallel work)
   - Estimated worker-sessions
   - Max parallelism
   - Warnings for potential issues

## Reference
~/gt/docs/swarm-architecture.md - 'The Ready Front Model' section

## Acceptance
- bd swarm validate <epic> runs
- Reports ready fronts accurately
- Warns about structural issues
- Returns success/failure exit code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:11:08.788203-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-28T19:34:57.218009-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T19:34:57.218009-08:00"}

Recommendation: Add optional debug logging when resurrection occurs, e.g., Issue bd-abc resurrected (tombstone expired).  {"id":"bd-nlzv","title":"bd show --short flag for compact output","description":"## Request
Add a `--short` flag to `bd show` for compact, one-line output per issue.

## Use Case
When checking multiple issues quickly:
```bash
bd show bd-nkqx --short && bd show bd-oxgi --short
```

Currently fails with "unknown flag: --short"

## Expected Output
Something like:
```
bd-nkqx [closed] P1 task: Validate swarm schema design
bd-oxgi [closed] P1 task: Add mol_type schema field to beads
```

## Format Suggestion
`<id> [<status>] <priority> <type>: <title>`

Optional: include blocker count if any, e.g. `(blocked by 2)`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-28T21:26:18.955592-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-28T21:35:06.006865-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T21:35:06.006865-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-x0zl","title":"Remove/relocate pin command (covered by mol commands)","description":"## Task
Review and potentially remove `bd pin` as it overlaps with mol commands.

## Analysis needed
- `bd pin` sets the pinned flag on issues for agent work assignment
- `bd hook` shows what's pinned to an agent
- `bd unpin` removes the pinned flag
- `bd mol` commands deal with molecules/templates

## Options
1. **Keep as-is**: pin/unpin are about work assignment, mol is about templates
2. **Move under mol**: `bd mol pin`, `bd mol unpin` 
3. **Deprecate**: If mol commands fully cover the use case

## Decision criteria
- Are pin/unpin used independently of mol workflows?
- Does Gas Town (gt) use these commands?

## Recommendation
Check gt codebase for usage patterns before deciding. If pin/unpin are primarily used with mol workflows, consolidate under mol. If used independently, keep as-is.

## Files potentially affected
- cmd/bd/pin.go
- cmd/bd/unpin.go
- cmd/bd/hook.go
- cmd/bd/mol.go (if moving)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-27T15:11:09.799471-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-27T16:01:53.437698-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T16:01:53.437698-08:00","comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-x0zl","author":"stevey","text":"## Analysis Complete

**Findings:**
- bd pin, bd unpin, bd hook are **never used** by gt
- gt uses bd update --status=pinned --assignee=agent instead (10 usages)
- Code comment says 'pinned field is cosmetic for bd hook visibility'
- gt mol status provides equivalent hook inspection

**Recommendation:** Remove all three commands as dead code.","created_at":"2025-12-28T00:00:14Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-x2ht","title":"Add BD_AGENT_MODE for ultra-compact output","description":"Optional agent-optimized output mode triggered by BD_AGENT_MODE=1 env var:

- Compact format: just ID and title per line
- Default to open/in_progress only
- No colors, no emojis
- Lower default limit (~20)
- One-line summaries

This is independent but benefits from TTY detection infrastructure.

Consider auto-detecting agent context via CLAUDE_CODE or similar env vars.","notes":"## Implementation Plan

### Trigger
```bash
BD_AGENT_MODE=1 bd list
```

Or auto-detect common agent environments:
```go
func IsAgentMode() bool {
    if os.Getenv("BD_AGENT_MODE") != "" {
        return true
    }
    // Auto-detect known agent contexts
    if os.Getenv("CLAUDE_CODE") != "" {
        return true
    }
    // Add other agent env vars as discovered
    return false
}
```

### Code Changes

1. **Add agent output format** (cmd/bd/list.go):
```go
if ui.IsAgentMode() {
    // Ultra-compact: just ID and title
    for _, issue := range issues {
        fmt.Printf("%s: %s\n", issue.ID, issue.Title)
    }
    return
}
```

2. **Agent mode defaults**:
```go
if ui.IsAgentMode() {
    if limit == 0 {
        limit = 20 // Lower default for agents
    }
    if status == "" {
        // Only actionable work
        filter.Status = []types.Status{"open", "in_progress"}
    }
}
```

### Output Format (Agent Mode)
```
bd-mypl: Default bd list to non-closed issues
bd-v5fn: Add default limit to bd list (50 issues)
bd-jdz3: Add pager support to bd list
```

No colors, no emojis, no brackets, no priority display.
Just ID + title for maximum context efficiency.

### Testing
- `BD_AGENT_MODE=1 bd list` outputs compact format
- Verify no ANSI codes in output
- Verify default limit is 20
- Verify only open/in_progress shown by default","status":"hooked","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/polecats/ruby","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:11.057467-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:49:04.301018-08:00"}

Consider auto-detecting agent context via CLAUDE_CODE or similar env vars.","notes":"## Implementation Plan

### Trigger
```bash
BD_AGENT_MODE=1 bd list
```

Or auto-detect common agent environments:
```go
func IsAgentMode() bool {
    if os.Getenv("BD_AGENT_MODE") != "" {
        return true
    }
    // Auto-detect known agent contexts
    if os.Getenv("CLAUDE_CODE") != "" {
        return true
    }
    // Add other agent env vars as discovered
    return false
}
```

### Code Changes

1. **Add agent output format** (cmd/bd/list.go):
```go
if ui.IsAgentMode() {
    // Ultra-compact: just ID and title
    for _, issue := range issues {
        fmt.Printf("%s: %s\n", issue.ID, issue.Title)
    }
    return
}
```

2. **Agent mode defaults**:
```go
if ui.IsAgentMode() {
    if limit == 0 {
        limit = 20 // Lower default for agents
    }
    if status == "" {
        // Only actionable work
        filter.Status = []types.Status{"open", "in_progress"}
    }
}
```

### Output Format (Agent Mode)
```
bd-mypl: Default bd list to non-closed issues
bd-v5fn: Add default limit to bd list (50 issues)
bd-jdz3: Add pager support to bd list
```

No colors, no emojis, no brackets, no priority display.
Just ID + title for maximum context efficiency.

### Testing

- Verify no ANSI codes in output
- Verify default limit is 20
- Verify only open/in_progress shown by default","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/polecats/ruby","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:11.057467-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:59:24.474412-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T15:59:24.474412-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: BD_AGENT_MODE=1 triggers ultra-compact output format"}

 {"id":"bd-x3hi","title":"Support redirect files in .beads/ directory","description":"Gas Town creates polecat worktrees with .beads/redirect files that point to a shared beads database. The bd CLI should:

1. When finding a .beads/ directory, check if it contains a 'redirect' file
2. If redirect exists, read the relative path and use that as the beads directory
3. This allows multiple git worktrees to share a single beads database

Example:
- polecats/alpha/.beads/redirect contains '../../mayor/rig/.beads'
- bd commands from alpha should use mayor/rig/.beads

Currently bd ignores redirect files and either uses the local .beads/ or walks up to find a parent .beads/.

Related: gt-nriy (test message that can't be retrieved due to missing redirect support)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:46:23.415172-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.759664-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.759664-08:00"}
